text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously constructive - tech/chips holding up, but the White House AI crackdown headline is a real headwind for your core names. That story matters most to you: any signal of export restrictions or regulatory tightening hits semis and cloud first.

Your holdings: PTNM is down -15.25% and sitting just above its $9.50 stop - it's a tiny spec on margin, one bad print away from stopping out, watch it closely. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history; margin amplifies any sudden drop hard. SOXL is a 3x leveraged ETF - it's up 1.77% but it decays daily; don't let a small gain turn into a real loss if semis wobble.

Watch: The AI crackdown story - if it escalates (new export rules, named companies), SMH and SOXL get hit fastest. Also watch SPCX for any IPO-lock-up or volatility spikes.

Bottom line: Protect your leveraged and spec positions first - margin means a 10% drop costs you double the pain.
plays: PTNM: Stop is at $9.50 - it's already -15.25% and on margin. Consider cutting or sizing way down now before it hits the stop automatically.
SOXL: Already own it; if semis soften on AI crackdown news, don't wait for $200 stop. Consider tightening stop toward $230 given 3x decay risk.
No new buys today - macro AI headline creates sector uncertainty; scanner setups exist but no clean catalyst to pull the trigger right now.
